goal: Review "specs\hestami-ai-real-property-os(2)\Hestami AI Real Property OS and Platform Product Description.md" and prepare for implementation.

finalizedPlan.title: Product Discovery for Hestami AI Real Property OS and Platform
finalizedPlan.summary: This plan outlines the product strategy for building the Hestami AI Real Property OS, a three-pillar platform to serve Homeowners, Service Providers, and Community Associations with an integrated, end-to-end solution for property management, maintenance, and governance.

requirements.text: The system must support a common data model that includes associations, properties, people, accounting, workflows, violations, ARC requests, work orders, communications, governance, documents, reserve studies, and compliance.
The accounting module must provide an auditable, compliant General Ledger (GL) structure.
The system must automatically post periodic assessments for community associations.
Work orders must integrate with Accounts Payable and payment processing.
Workflows for violations must support evidence, notice sequences, hearings, and fine-to-GL posting.
Architectural Request (ARC) workflows must manage plans, committee reviews, and conditions.
The platform must provide portals for Owners, Tenants, Managers, and Vendors with role-based access.
The system must feature AI-driven extraction, classification, and summarization for documents and records.
The system must provide dedicated native mobile clients for iOS and Android for homeowners and field technicians.
Mobile clients must support task-centric workflows and have offline-first capabilities.

decisions.text: The product will be built in three phases, corresponding to the three pillars: 1) Home Assistant, 2) Service Provider FSM, 3) Community Association Management.
The Service Provider Field Services Management pillar will be functionally modeled after ServiceTitan.
The Community Association Management pillar will be functionally modeled after Vantaca.
The technical stack is pre-defined, including SvelteKit (Frontend), Node.js/Bun (Backend), DBOS (Workflow), PostgreSQL (DB), and Cerbos (AuthZ).

constraints.text: Cloudflare CDN must be the only public entry point to the system; no direct client access to the origin IP is permitted.
Durable, multi-step processes must be implemented as DBOS versioned workflows.
Strict multi-tenant data isolation must be enforced at the database level using PostgreSQL's Row-Level Security (RLS).
External integrations, such as with Nextdoor, are out of scope for the initial implementation.

openQuestions.text: Pillar 2 (FSM) is modeled on ServiceTitan. Do the proposed user journeys (UJ-6 to UJ-10) accurately reflect the desired core feature set for Phase 2?
Pillar 3 (CAM) is modeled on Vantaca. Do the proposed user journeys (UJ-11 to UJ-15) accurately reflect the desired core feature set for Phase 3?
Within each phase, what is the relative priority of the user journeys? For Phase 1, should we prioritize new service calls over property portfolio management, for example?
The document mentions AI-driven features like document analysis and statutory logic parsing. What are the specific, measurable goals for these AI features in their initial versions?

finalizedPlan.proposedApproach: The implementation will follow a three-phased approach, building one pillar of the platform at a time: Home Assistant, Service Provider FSM, and finally Community Association Management. The entire system will be built on a pre-defined, modern technology stack consisting of SvelteKit for the frontend, Node.js/Bun for backend services, PostgreSQL as the primary database, DBOS for durable workflow orchestration, and Cerbos for authorization. Architecturally, the system will enforce strict security and data-handling patterns: all public traffic will be routed through Cloudflare CDN, multi-tenancy will be enforced at the database level using PostgreSQL's Row-Level Security, and all complex, multi-step business processes like violations or ARC requests will be implemented as versioned, durable workflows in DBOS. A central focus will be the development of the common data model specified in REQ-1, as it underpins the integration between the three pillars.
finalizedPlan.productVision: To create the integrated, holistic, end-to-end AI operating system for home services, matching property owners and service providers through digital asset exchange to minimize effort for all parties.
finalizedPlan.productDescription: The Hestami AI Real Property OS and Platform is a comprehensive, three-pillar system. Pillar 1, the 'Home Real Property Assistant', empowers homeowners and small landlords to efficiently manage property maintenance by finding, vetting, and coordinating with contractors. Pillar 2, 'Service Provider Field Services Management', provides an all-in-one 'operating system' for trade businesses to manage scheduling, dispatch, invoicing, and customer relations. Pillar 3, 'Community Association Management', offers a robust platform for HOAs to handle governance, financials, compliance, and resident services. The entire system is built on a shared data model and a modern tech stack to deliver a seamless, integrated experience.
successMetrics: For Pillar 1: Time from service call creation to vendor selection.
For Pillar 1: Percentage of homeowners who use the platform for a second service call.
For Pillar 2: Reduction in time spent on scheduling and invoicing for service providers.
For Pillar 2: Adoption rate of the technician mobile app.
For Pillar 3: Reduction in time spent on manual accounting tasks (e.g., invoice processing) for CAMs.
For Pillar 3: Homeowner and Board member satisfaction with portal usability and transparency.
Platform-wide: Net Promoter Score (NPS) from all user personas.

phasingStrategy.description: Pillar 1: Home Real Property Assistant for homeowners and small landlords.
phasingStrategy.rationale: Establishes the foundational property owner-facing functionality.

phasingStrategy.description: Pillar 2: Service Provider Field Services Management (FSM).
phasingStrategy.rationale: Builds the supply-side of the marketplace, modeled after ServiceTitan.
Completes the platform by adding governance and management for HOAs, modeled after Vantaca.

uxRequirements: Security: The platform must be compliant with OWASP Top 10 security risks, with data encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.2+).
Security: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) must be strictly enforced for all API endpoints and data access.
Compliance: Financial transactions must adhere to PCI-DSS standards by delegating cardholder data management to a certified payment gateway.
Compliance: User data handling must comply with data privacy regulations such as GDPR and CCPA, including rights to access and erasure.
Performance: API response times for interactive user queries must average below 250ms under the 95th percentile (p95) load.
Performance: The platform must support an initial target of 1,000 concurrent active users without degradation.
Scalability: The infrastructure must be designed to scale horizontally to accommodate growth in users, properties, and service requests.
Availability: The platform must maintain a Service Level Objective (SLO) of 99.9% uptime, excluding scheduled maintenance windows.
Availability: A comprehensive disaster recovery plan must be in place, with automated database backups performed at least daily and retained for 30 days.
Usability: The user interface must be compliant with Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1 Level AA.
Usability: The application must be fully responsive and provide a consistent user experience across major web browsers and mobile devices.
Data Integrity: All financial and status-change operations must be transactional to prevent data corruption or inconsistency.
Maintainability: The system will be architected with clear boundaries between domains to promote modularity and independent development.
Maintainability: A CI/CD pipeline must be implemented to automate testing and deployment, ensuring code quality and rapid iteration.

domainProposals.name: Property Management
domainProposals.description: This domain covers the core functionality related to managing individual properties, including their detailed information, ownership, and digital representations.
domainProposals.rationale: Directly implied by '[Phase 1] Onboard a New Property' and '[Phase 1] Manage a Portfolio of Investment Properties' where homeowners interact with property addresses, ownership details, and property-specific service calls. Source: user-specified

domainProposals.name: Digital Asset Management
domainProposals.description: Focuses on the storage, processing, and retrieval of various digital assets associated with properties and service requests, such as photos, videos, and documents.
domainProposals.rationale: Explicitly mentioned in '[Phase 1] Onboard a New Property' (uploads property photos, videos, documents) and '[Phase 1] Create a Service Call' (describes issue with photos, video). Also supports the vision of 'digital asset exchange'. Source: user-specified

domainProposals.name: Service Request Management
domainProposals.description: Manages the lifecycle of service calls for property repairs and improvements, from creation and description to status tracking and resolution.
domainProposals.rationale: Central to '[Phase 1] Create a Service Call for a Repair' and relevant across multiple other journeys. It's the core mechanism for homeowners to initiate work. Source: user-specified

domainProposals.name: AI & Intelligent Automation
domainProposals.description: Utilizes artificial intelligence to analyze requests, suggest solutions, match service providers, and automate various platform processes to enhance efficiency and accuracy.
domainProposals.rationale: Explicitly stated in '[Phase 1] Create a Service Call for a Repair' (AI to analyze request, suggest service types) and implied in 'Facilitate Vendor Matching' and the overall product vision of an 'AI operating system'. Source: user-specified

domainProposals.name: Vendor Management & Procurement
domainProposals.description: Handles the discovery, qualification, bidding, and engagement of service providers/contractors for fulfilling service requests.
domainProposals.rationale: Crucial for '[Phase 1] Facilitate Vendor Matching for a Service Call' and 'Create a Service Call for a Repair' (initiate vendor discovery). It connects the supply and demand for home services. Source: user-specified

domainProposals.name: Community Association Management (HOA)
domainProposals.description: Specifically addresses the unique requirements of Homeowners Associations, including approval processes and communication with HOA entities.
domainProposals.rationale: Directly specified in '[Phase 1] Track an External HOA Approval' where HOA approval letters are uploaded and tracked. Source: user-specified

domainProposals.name: Portfolio Management
domainProposals.description: Allows property owners, particularly investors, to view and manage multiple properties and their associated service activities from a consolidated perspective.
domainProposals.rationale: Explicitly defined by '[Phase 1] Manage a Portfolio of Investment Properties' covering dashboards and cross-property service call management. Source: user-specified
workflowPreview: Initiate Service Call for Specific Portfolio Property

domainProposals.name: User & Access Management
domainProposals.description: Manages user accounts, authentication, roles, and permissions across the platform for all personas.
domainProposals.rationale: Essential for any multi-user platform with distinct personas (Homeowner, Service Provider, CAM, HOA Board Member, Hestami Staff) needing secure and role-based access to features and data. This is foundational to the accepted personas interacting with the system. Source: ai-proposed

domainProposals.name: Financial Management
domainProposals.description: Encompasses invoicing, payments, billing, and financial tracking for services rendered and received through the platform.
domainProposals.rationale: While not explicitly detailed in Phase 1, the 'end-to-end' nature of an 'operating system for home services' and the transaction of matching service providers implies the need for payment processing and financial record-keeping. Source: ai-proposed

domainProposals.name: Communication & Notifications
domainProposals.description: Facilitates seamless communication between all parties (homeowners, service providers, Hestami staff, CAMs) and delivers timely notifications regarding service call updates, bids, and approvals.
domainProposals.rationale: The platform acts as a 'central hub of communication' (CAM persona) and requires interaction for 'solicit interest and bids'. An 'integrated, holistic' system needs robust communication channels beyond just status updates. Source: ai-proposed

domainProposals.name: Scheduling & Logistics
domainProposals.description: Manages the scheduling of service appointments, dispatching of technicians, and tracking of service timelines and availability.
domainProposals.rationale: Once vendors are matched and bids accepted, the practical execution of services requires scheduling and managing appointments, which is a core component of 'home services'. Source: ai-proposed

domainProposals.name: Reporting & Analytics
domainProposals.description: Provides dashboards and reports with actionable insights for homeowners, CAMs, HOA Board Members, and Hestami staff to monitor property health, service performance, and operational efficiency.
domainProposals.rationale: Explicitly mentioned in '[Phase 1] Manage a Portfolio of Investment Properties' (views a dashboard). Also crucial for 'HOA Board Member' who 'need clear information to make informed decisions'. Source: user-specified

domainProposals.name: Platform Administration & Configuration
domainProposals.description: Provides tools and interfaces for Hestami staff to manage the platform itself, including system settings, user configurations, data integrity, and compliance.
domainProposals.rationale: The 'Hestami Staff / Admin' persona is responsible for 'platform administration'. This domain ensures the system operates smoothly, securely, and is configurable. Source: ai-proposed

domainProposals.name: Security & Data Privacy
domainProposals.description: Ensures the security of user data, transactions, and platform operations, while adhering to relevant data privacy regulations and industry standards.
domainProposals.rationale: Essential for any platform handling sensitive property and personal data. Implicit in the need for a trusted 'operating system' and 'fiduciary duty' of HOA Board Members. Source: ai-proposed

domainProposals.name: Integration Services
domainProposals.description: Manages connections and data exchange with external third-party systems, such as smart home devices, accounting software, or other service marketplaces.
domainProposals.rationale: The vision of an 'integrated, holistic, end-to-end AI operating system' implies future extensibility and connectivity with other services and devices in the home ecosystem. Source: ai-proposed

entityProposals.name: User
entityProposals.description: Represents an individual who interacts with the platform, such as a homeowner, service provider, CAM, or admin.

entityProposals.name: Role
entityProposals.description: Defines a set of permissions for a user type, such as 'Homeowner', 'ServiceProviderAdmin', 'Technician', 'CAM', 'HOABoardMember', 'HestamiAdmin'.

entityProposals.name: Permission
entityProposals.description: Represents a specific action that can be performed on the platform, e.g., 'create:serviceRequest', 'approve:invoice', 'view:portfolioDashboard'.

entityProposals.name: Property
entityProposals.description: Represents a single real estate property, such as a house, condo, or a community common area.

entityProposals.name: Property Asset
entityProposals.description: Represents a specific piece of equipment or major component within a property, like an HVAC unit, water heater, or appliance, to track its service history.

entityProposals.name: Digital Asset
entityProposals.description: Stores metadata for any uploaded file, such as photos of damage, videos, invoices, or compliance documents.

entityProposals.name: Service Request
entityProposals.description: The central entity for tracking a request for repair, maintenance, or improvement from creation to completion.

entityProposals.name: Feedback
entityProposals.description: Represents a rating and review left by a homeowner for a service provider after a job is completed.

entityProposals.name: Service Request History
entityProposals.description: An audit log of all status changes and key events in the lifecycle of a service request.

entityProposals.name: AI Triage Result
entityProposals.description: Stores the output from the AI Service Request Triage workflow, including category, priority, and suggested actions.

entityProposals.name: Vendor Recommendation
entityProposals.description: Stores the list of vendors suggested by the Intelligent Vendor Matching workflow for a specific service request.

entityProposals.name: Service Provider
entityProposals.description: Represents a contracting business or individual vendor available on the platform.

entityProposals.name: Technician
entityProposals.description: Represents an individual field technician who works for a Service Provider.

entityProposals.name: Bid
entityProposals.description: A formal offer from a Service Provider to complete a Service Request for a certain price and timeframe.

entityProposals.name: Compliance Document
entityProposals.description: Tracks the status and details of required documents for vendors, like insurance certificates or licenses.

entityProposals.name: Community
entityProposals.description: Represents a Homeowners Association (HOA) or other managed community.

entityProposals.name: Architectural Request
entityProposals.description: A formal submission by a homeowner for approval of a modification to their property, governed by the HOA.

entityProposals.name: Vote
entityProposals.description: Records the decision of an HOA board member on a high-cost proposal or architectural request.

entityProposals.name: Portfolio
entityProposals.description: A logical grouping of multiple properties managed by a single user, typically an investor.

entityProposals.name: Invoice
entityProposals.description: A detailed bill for a completed service, including line items, total amount, and due date.

entityProposals.name: Payment
entityProposals.description: Records a financial transaction from a homeowner to pay for a service.

entityProposals.name: Payout
entityProposals.description: Records a financial disbursement from the platform to a Service Provider for a completed job.

entityProposals.name: Payment Method
entityProposals.description: Stores a tokenized representation of a user's payment method, such as a credit card or bank account.

entityProposals.name: Notification
entityProposals.description: A record of a notification (email, SMS, push) sent to a user as part of an automated cascade or other communication.

entityProposals.name: Announcement
entityProposals.description: Represents a message, such as a community newsletter or maintenance alert, sent to a group of users.

entityProposals.name: Appointment
entityProposals.description: A scheduled time slot for a service to be performed.

entityProposals.name: Dispatch
entityProposals.description: Assigns a specific technician to a specific appointment or service request.

entityProposals.name: Schedule
entityProposals.description: Defines the working hours and availability for a service provider or an individual technician.

entityProposals.name: Performance Metric
entityProposals.description: Stores calculated performance data for service providers, such as average rating, response time, and job completion rate.

entityProposals.name: Audit Log
entityProposals.description: A system-wide log that records significant actions performed by users or automated processes for security and compliance.

workflowProposals.name: AI Service Request Triage
workflowProposals.description: Automates the classification and prioritization of incoming service requests using AI.
steps: A new service request is submitted by a user.
Natural Language Processing (NLP) analyzes the text description to identify keywords and intent.
Image recognition analyzes uploaded photos/videos to identify objects and potential issues.
The system assigns a service category (e.g., Plumbing, Electrical), sub-category (e.g., Leaky Faucet), and priority level (e.g., Emergency, High, Normal).
The system suggests potential quick-fix solutions to the user.
The classified and prioritized request is routed to the dispatch queue.
triggers: Creation of a new service request.

workflowProposals.name: Intelligent Vendor Matching
workflowProposals.description: Automatically identifies and suggests the best-fit service providers for a given job.
steps: A new service request is triaged and ready for dispatch.
The system queries the vendor database based on service category, sub-category, and geographic service area.
An AI model ranks the matching vendors based on a weighted score including availability, average user rating, past performance on similar jobs, and price competitiveness.
The top 3-5 ranked vendors are notified of the job lead.
If no vendors accept within a set timeframe, the system automatically notifies the next group of ranked vendors.
triggers: A service request reaching the 'Ready to Dispatch' status.

workflowProposals.name: Service Status Notification Cascade
workflowProposals.description: Automates the delivery of status updates to all relevant parties throughout the lifecycle of a service request.
steps: The status of a service request changes.
The system identifies all subscribed parties (e.g., Homeowner, CAM, assigned Service Provider).
The system formats a predefined message template for the specific status change (e.g., 'Your technician is on the way!').
The message is delivered via each user's preferred notification channels (e.g., push notification, email, SMS).
triggers: Any change in the 'status' field of a service request record.

workflowProposals.name: Invoice Payment and Payout
workflowProposals.description: Manages the automated process of homeowner payment and service provider payout.
steps: A service provider generates an invoice for a completed job.
The homeowner is notified and pays the invoice via the platform's payment gateway.
The payment gateway confirms successful transaction.
The system deducts the platform service fee from the total amount.
The remaining funds are queued for payout to the service provider.
The system initiates an ACH transfer to the service provider's linked bank account based on the payout schedule (e.g., daily, weekly).
A receipt is issued to the homeowner and a payout confirmation is sent to the provider.
triggers: Successful payment of an invoice by a homeowner.

workflowProposals.name: HOA Architectural Request Approval
workflowProposals.description: Automates the routing and review process for homeowner architectural modification requests.
steps: A homeowner submits a new architectural request.
The system assigns it a tracking number and notifies the CAM for initial review.
The CAM reviews for completeness. If incomplete, it's returned to the homeowner with comments. If complete, the CAM forwards it to the Architectural Review Committee (ARC).
ARC members are notified and given a deadline to review and vote.
The system tracks votes and comments from ARC members.
Once the deadline passes or all have voted, the system determines the final decision.
The CAM is notified of the decision to perform a final review.
The system sends the official decision letter to the homeowner.
triggers: Submission of a new architectural request.

workflowProposals.name: Vendor Compliance Monitoring
workflowProposals.description: Automatically tracks the status of vendor compliance documents, such as insurance certificates.
steps: The system runs a daily check on all active vendor profiles.
For each vendor, it checks the expiration dates of key documents (e.g., General Liability Insurance, Worker's Comp).
If a document is set to expire within 30 days, the system sends an automated reminder email to the vendor.
Reminders are sent again at 15 days and 7 days.
If a document expires, the system automatically flags the vendor's profile as 'Non-Compliant' and temporarily suspends them from receiving new job leads.
The system notifies Hestami Staff of the suspension.
Once the vendor uploads a new, valid document, the system automatically lifts the suspension.

integrationProposals.name: Payment Gateway
integrationProposals.description: Processes payments from homeowners for services and facilitates payouts to service providers. Handles tokenization of payment methods to ensure PCI compliance.
integrationProposals.rationale: Essential for handling all financial transactions securely and reliably within the platform, as specified in the Financial Management domain.

integrationProposals.name: Accounting Software Sync
integrationProposals.description: Synchronizes invoices, payments, and payout records with external accounting platforms.
integrationProposals.rationale: Automates bookkeeping and financial reporting for platform administrators and can be offered as a feature to service providers.

integrationProposals.name: Transactional Email Service
integrationProposals.description: Sends automated emails for notifications, announcements, alerts, and summaries.
integrationProposals.rationale: Provides a scalable and reliable mechanism for all email-based communication, a core part of the Communication & Notifications domain.

integrationProposals.name: SMS Notification Service
integrationProposals.description: Sends text message notifications for time-sensitive events like appointment reminders or dispatch alerts.
integrationProposals.rationale: Enables immediate communication with users for critical updates, supporting the notification cascade workflow.

integrationProposals.name: Mobile Push Notification Service
integrationProposals.description: Sends push notifications to users' mobile devices to provide real-time updates and alerts.
integrationProposals.rationale: Crucial for engaging users of the mobile application and delivering instant status changes, as per the Communication domain.

integrationProposals.name: Identity & Authentication Provider
integrationProposals.description: Manages user authentication, including password management, social logins (Google, Apple), and Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA).
integrationProposals.rationale: Secures user access to the platform and simplifies the login process, which is foundational to the User & Access Management domain.

integrationProposals.name: Cloud File Storage
integrationProposals.description: Stores and serves all user-uploaded binary files, such as photos, videos, and compliance documents.
integrationProposals.rationale: Provides a scalable, secure, and highly available solution for managing digital assets, a core requirement of the Digital Asset Management domain.

integrationProposals.name: Generative AI & NLP Service
integrationProposals.description: Provides natural language processing and image analysis capabilities to power intelligent features.
integrationProposals.rationale: Directly enables the 'AI Service Request Triage' and 'Intelligent Vendor Matching' workflows, which are central to the product's value proposition.

integrationProposals.name: Geospatial & Mapping Service
integrationProposals.description: Provides address validation, geocoding (address to coordinates), reverse geocoding, and mapping functionalities.
integrationProposals.rationale: Enhances property management and scheduling/logistics by enabling location visualization, route planning for technicians, and address verification.

integrationProposals.name: Public Property Data API
integrationProposals.description: Retrieves public records data for real estate properties, such as assessed value, year built, square footage, and ownership history.
integrationProposals.rationale: Automates the population of property details, ensuring data accuracy and improving the user experience for property managers and homeowners.

integrationProposals.name: External Calendar Sync
integrationProposals.description: Allows users to synchronize appointments and schedules from the platform with their personal or work calendars.
integrationProposals.rationale: Improves user experience and schedule adherence for both homeowners and technicians within the Scheduling & Logistics domain.

integrationProposals.name: Background Check & Verification
integrationProposals.description: Conducts background checks on service providers and verifies professional licenses and certifications.
integrationProposals.rationale: Essential for the 'Vendor Compliance Monitoring' workflow, building trust and safety on the platform.

integrationProposals.name: HOA/CAM Management Software Sync
integrationProposals.description: Integrates with existing software used by Community Association Managers (CAMs) to synchronize data on residents, financials, and architectural requests.
integrationProposals.rationale: Drives adoption by property management companies by fitting into their existing software ecosystem, crucial for the Community Association Management domain.

integrationProposals.name: Business Intelligence Platform
integrationProposals.description: Feeds platform data into a BI tool for advanced analytics, custom dashboard creation, and in-depth reporting.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports the Reporting & Analytics domain by enabling powerful data exploration for platform administrators and power users.

integrationProposals.name: Centralized Logging & Monitoring
integrationProposals.description: Aggregates application logs, monitors system performance, and provides security information and event management (SIEM) capabilities.
integrationProposals.rationale: Critical for maintaining platform health, diagnosing issues, and ensuring security and compliance across all domains.

qualityAttributes: Security: The platform must be compliant with OWASP Top 10 security risks, with data encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.2+).
Security: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) must be strictly enforced for all API endpoints and data access.
Compliance: Financial transactions must adhere to PCI-DSS standards by delegating cardholder data management to a certified payment gateway.
Compliance: User data handling must comply with data privacy regulations such as GDPR and CCPA, including rights to access and erasure.
Performance: API response times for interactive user queries must average below 250ms under the 95th percentile (p95) load.
Performance: The platform must support an initial target of 1,000 concurrent active users without degradation.
Scalability: The infrastructure must be designed to scale horizontally to accommodate growth in users, properties, and service requests.
Availability: The platform must maintain a Service Level Objective (SLO) of 99.9% uptime, excluding scheduled maintenance windows.
Availability: A comprehensive disaster recovery plan must be in place, with automated database backups performed at least daily and retained for 30 days.
Usability: The user interface must be compliant with Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1 Level AA.
Usability: The application must be fully responsive and provide a consistent user experience across major web browsers and mobile devices.
Data Integrity: All financial and status-change operations must be transactional to prevent data corruption or inconsistency.
Maintainability: The system will be architected with clear boundaries between domains to promote modularity and independent development.
Maintainability: A CI/CD pipeline must be implemented to automate testing and deployment, ensuring code quality and rapid iteration.

openLoops.description: Definition of specific, measurable goals for AI features (document analysis, statutory logic parsing) in their initial versions.
Validation of whether the proposed user journeys (UJ-6 to UJ-15) for Pillars 2 and 3 accurately reflect the desired feature sets for those phases.
Relative priority of user journeys within Phase 1 (e.g., prioritizing new service calls vs. property portfolio management).
